drag.revealText: EDITH'S HAND, UNDER THE VIOLET LIGHT: 'She came again today. I counted the knives after she left. I am not being dramatic. I counted them twice.' [PLACEHOLDER — Edith counter-narration sequence]

hotspots.examine: Her coat rack. She never owned enough coats to fill it. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A coat rack. Four hooks, all worn.

hotspots.examine: The mail's been piling up. Bills, mostly. I should have come sooner. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: Unopened mail. The postmarks span eleven weeks.

hotspots.examine: The hallway light. It always flickered. Edith found it charming. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A light switch. The plate around it is clean; the wall is not.

hotspots.examine: Just my old boots. I left them here years ago and she kept them. Sentimental. [PLACEHOLDER LIE]
hotspots.lookAgain: Two pairs of shoes, different sizes. Both have fresh mud at the heel.

hotspots.examine: Edith's reading chair. She'd sit here every night with her tea. She was so at peace in this apartment. [PLACEHOLDER LIE]

examineTiered.text: The chair again? It's just a chair, sweetheart. [PLACEHOLDER DEFENSIVE]

hotspots.lookAgain: A chair facing the wall, not the window. Deep scratches on the armrests. The floor beneath is worn through the varnish in two parallel tracks.

hotspots.examine: The cushion's gone flat. She really did live in this chair. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: Something rigid under the cushion. A key ring: five keys.

hotspots.examine: The floors need refinishing. Old buildings. [PLACEHOLDER — hints at cushion]
hotspots.lookAgain: Two parallel gouges run from the chair to the wall. Something heavy was dragged, many times. They pass under the chair cushion.

hotspots.examine: Our photo wall. That's us at the lake house — she's laughing because I'd just dropped the thermos. God, that day. [PLACEHOLDER LIE]

examineTiered.text: You've seen the photos already. Why do you keep counting them? [PLACEHOLDER DEFENSIVE]

hotspots.lookAgain: Six frames. Five photographs. The sixth frame is empty; its glass is clean except for four fingerprints.

hotspots.examine: The answering machine, blinking away. Let's hear it... 'Edith, it's the pharmacy again—' Old messages. Nothing important. [PLACEHOLDER — plays machine]
hotspots.lookAgain: Fourteen messages. Thirteen are from the same number. The counter says they were played before, all of them, recently.

hotspots.examine: Her old phone. She refused a mobile. I called every Sunday. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A corded phone. The emergency numbers card beside it has a new number written over an old one, in different ink.

hotspots.examine: Her teacup, still out. Chamomile, always. I can almost smell it. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A cup with a dried ring inside. It smells of nothing.

hotspots.examine: Her books. Mysteries, mostly. She always guessed the ending by chapter three. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: The mysteries are shelved spine-in for one full row. Someone did not want those titles read.

hotspots.examine: The window she loved. South light all afternoon. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: The curtain hem is nailed to the sill.

hotspots.examine: The packing boxes. We're nearly done, you and I. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A packing box, half-filled. The tape gun is warm.

exits.lockedText: The kitchen can wait. I want to finish in here first. Play her messages — you should hear her friends' voices. [PLACEHOLDER LOCK]

hotspots.examine: Her wall calendar. Look — 'Mara visits' every single week, in her handwriting. We were close like that. [PLACEHOLDER LIE — puzzle 1/3]
hotspots.lookAgain: A calendar in two hands. The 'Mara visits' entries continue for six weeks past the date on the memorial card taped to the fridge.

hotspots.examine: I should empty the fridge before it turns. She always kept it stocked for guests. [PLACEHOLDER — puzzle 2/3]
hotspots.lookAgain: Milk dated two weeks ago. Eggs, half gone. Someone has been shopping. Someone has been eating.

hotspots.examine: One plate, one cup — she ate alone, poor thing. I hate that she ate alone. [PLACEHOLDER LIE — puzzle 3/3]
hotspots.lookAgain: Two plates. Two cups. Two forks, still wet.

hotspots.examine: Her pills. The doctors kept changing the dosage. She managed it all herself, so organized. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A seven-day organizer, all fourteen lids closed, all fourteen wells full. Dust on the Monday lid.

hotspots.examine: The kettle. Chamomile, always chamomile. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: The kettle is descaled and full. The chamomile box is empty; the coffee jar beside it is new.

hotspots.examine: Every kitchen has one drawer like this. Batteries, string, takeaway menus. Nothing worth keeping. [PLACEHOLDER — notebook found here]
hotspots.lookAgain: Under the menus: a notebook, warped by water. The cover says E. in pencil. Most pages are illegible — the ink only ghosts.

exits.lockedText: There's nothing in the bathroom but sad little bottles. Stay with me here a moment — look at her calendar, her fridge, her dishes. Help me remember her properly. [PLACEHOLDER LOCK]

hotspots.examine: Her little violet nightlight. For her eyes, she said. It made the whole room glow like a bruise. [PLACEHOLDER — drag target]
hotspots.lookAgain: A UV nightlight, plugged in, warm. Under its glow, the grout shows handprints the overhead light cannot see.

hotspots.examine: Her medicine cabinet. She was on top of all of it — I made sure. I'd count the pills with her on Sundays. [PLACEHOLDER LIE]

examineTiered.text: You keep looking at everything twice. Edith used to do that. [PLACEHOLDER META]

hotspots.lookAgain: Prescription bottles, unopened, seals intact. The fill dates stop eleven weeks ago.

hotspots.examine: Her sink. One toothbrush, see? Just hers. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: One toothbrush, bone dry. The soap bar is worn to a sliver and still slick.

hotspots.examine: Fresh towels. She kept a proper home to the very end. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: Two towels on the rail. One is damp.

hotspots.examine: The mirror. I look tired. This week has been so hard. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A mirror. It shows the room behind you. The door in the reflection is open wider than the door.

exits.lockedText: That's just storage, sweetheart. Boxes and dust. There's nothing of Edith in there. [PLACEHOLDER LOCK — the biggest lie]

hotspots.examine: See? Storage. That old bed is just for guests we never had. [PLACEHOLDER LIE]
hotspots.lookAgain: A made bed. The pillow holds the shape of a head. The sheets smell of detergent — washed this week.

hotspots.examine: Old dust sheets, probably. I'll sort them later. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A laundry basket. Folded clothes, adult-sized, none of them Edith's style. The top shirt is inside out, the way someone in a hurry undresses.

hotspots.examine: Boxes. I told you — storage. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A rail of hangers, half empty. On the floor, a sleeping bag, rolled tight, recently used.

hotspots.examine: Edith's old sewing desk. She never touched it in years. [PLACEHOLDER]
hotspots.lookAgain: A desk. In the drawer-well, a Polaroid camera. The counter window shows 1.

hotspots.examine: Take a picture, then. If it helps you say goodbye. Use the last one. Frame it kindly. [PLACEHOLDER — takes the photo, starts finale]
hotspots.lookAgain: Through the viewfinder the room is smaller. You press the shutter. The camera whines and gives up its last photograph, blank and developing.

beats: So now you know the whole of it. She was tired, and she was ill, and she went gently, in her own bed, in her own time. [PLACEHOLDER BEAT 1]
I visited every week. I brought her tea. I counted her pills. Nobody could have done more for her than I did. [PLACEHOLDER BEAT 2]
And I am certain — certain — that she never spent one night of her life afraid. [PLACEHOLDER BEAT 3 — accusation word lives here]
endingA: You pack the last box. Mara thanks you for helping her say goodbye. [PLACEHOLDER]
Over the credits, the Polaroid develops. The framing is wrong. There are two shadows. [PLACEHOLDER STINGER]

corrupt: The calendar kept counting after she stopped.
endingB.mirrorLookAgain: A mirror. You look again. [PLACEHOLDER — final objective description, writing pass required. Design requirement: the only objective description the player wishes had been narration.]
